question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addContractTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user set contractTransactionPredicate to null (via setContractTransactionPredicate(null)) to cause addContractTransaction() to always return early, preventing contract transactions from being recorded and hiding unauthorized contract interactions from the audit trail?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: addContractTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that addContractTransaction() records the payerAccountId from the RecordItem (derived from TransactionID without signature verification) as the payer of a contract transaction, allowing the attacker to falsely attribute contract execution to an account they do not control?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TransactionBody with DATA_NOT_SET and exactly one unknown field to cause parseTransactionType() to return that unknown field number as the transaction type, allowing the transaction to be classified as an arbitrary type (including privileged types) without the corresponding authorization checks?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TransactionBody with multiple unknown fields to cause parseTransactionType() to log an error and return DATA_NOT_SET (0), causing the transaction to be processed with an undefined type that may bypass type-specific authorization checks?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTransactionHash() returns the Ethereum transaction hash if ethereumTransaction is non-null (set via @Setter) to inject a crafted hash that does not correspond to the actual transaction bytes, breaking transaction integrity verification and allowing hash-based authorization lookups to be spoofed?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: build()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RecordItem where transactionBody is pre-set (non-null) and signatureMap is pre-set (non-null) to bypass the parseTransaction() call entirely (due to the null check on line 412), injecting an arbitrary TransactionBody and SignatureMap combination that was never validated against each other?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a SignedTransaction protobuf where bodyBytes parses to a valid TransactionBody but the sigMap contains signatures over a completely different body, causing RecordItem to store a TransactionBody that was never actually signed by the claimed signers, authorizing actions the signers never approved?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that parseTransaction() stores signatureMap from the SignedTransaction without verifying that any signature in the map actually covers the bodyBytes, allowing a transaction with a recycled/replayed signature map from a different transaction to be accepted as authorized?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: isChild()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction record that sets hasParentConsensusTimestamp() to true with an arbitrary timestamp to force isChild() to return true, causing the transaction to be treated as a child of a privileged parent and inherit that parent's authorization context without holding any signing keys?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: isTopLevel()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction with nonce = 0 in the TransactionID to always force isTopLevel() to return true regardless of whether parentConsensusTimestamp is set, bypassing child-transaction authorization checks that depend on isTopLevel() returning false?
